calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Britta Peterson for Joseph Helson, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The Court acknowledged receipt of the petitioner's brief addressing the probate examiner's notes. Mr. Peterson confirmed that the decedent and five grantees held equal co-ownership of the subject property, and the petition seeks confirmation of a 1/6 undivided interest as tenants in common. She explained that the family members had purchased the home for their parents, with each parent holding a 1/6 interest, and that they treated it as community property. The goal is to clear title for distribution through Eusebia Helsen's estate, which remains open in this Court.

The Court inquired further into the community property characterization of the interest held by the decedent and spouse. Mr. Peterson cited Family Code Section 2581 and Probate Code Section 28, arguing that property acquired during marriage with community property funds is presumed to be community property, absent a clear written statement designating it as separate property. She argued that the deed does not contain any language rebutting the community property presumption, that there are no written agreements or other evidence suggesting the property was intended to be separate property, and that all children are aware of the petition and no objections have been raised.

After discussions, the court took the matter under submission for further review of the petitioner's brief and the relevant case law.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
